description: Gene Rutherford called me over to look at an Empid he felt was likely an Alder. About 30 feet in front of us was a largish empid, with a large tail (moderately long and somewhat broad). The back was moderately bright green as was the crown. The wings were blackish with two bright off-white wingbars and white tertial edges that contrasted markedly with the "body" of the tertials. The secondaries were also brightly edged. The primary extension was long. The crown was low and gently rounded. The bill was medium-sized for an Empid (impression, not looked at closely), dark above, some pink/orange/yellow below. The green crown extended thru the auriculars with some gray hues. This contrasted sharply with a white throat. The eyering was very narrow, white, and round. The throat was white with some yellow wash to belly. The bird did not move tail or wings much when perched. After the initial 30 second or so look, I relocated the bird a few minutes later for about an equal amount of time, at which time it gave a clear pleep or peep sound consistent with Alder Flys I've hear in past
